mechanism_narrative: Elevated and rising FEDFUNDS levels raise the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing persistent negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains above its 12-month moving trend.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% in Dec 2022, up sharply from near-zero in early 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return is not significantly negative (p>0.05) over any rolling 3-year window, or positive returns occur in >55% of high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals an inflecting labor market that precedes broader earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as markets reprice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in Nov 2022, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly indicate a synchronized consumer and housing demand slowdown that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1425k in Oct 2022, down from 2022 peaks amid rate hikes
failure_condition: Joint-decline signal produces forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (p>0.05) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above its 12-month rolling average), a deceleration in INDPRO signals that tight policy is choking industrial output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; the same INDPRO deceleration in an easing regime is not predictive because it reflects benign productivity normalization rather than policy-induced contraction.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 12-month moving average and INDPRO MoM decline
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5175 with FEDFUNDS at 3.78% in a rapid hiking cycle
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during tightening regimes while remaining flat during easing regimes, contradicting the regime-dependence claim.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market strength and consumer income support, generating a short-term positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as it confirms economic resilience without triggering recession fears.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153,548k in Nov 2022, continuing steady monthly gains
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months show forward 5-day SPX returns not significantly different from zero or negative on average (t-test p>0.05) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains above 6% (a level that keeps the Fed committed to restrictive policy), equity markets price in continued rate hikes and multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until inflation shows sustained deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.349 in Nov 2022, implying YoY inflation still well above Fed target
failure_condition: During periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 6%, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances or show no significant negative mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST occurring while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously above 3%, reflects rate-driven housing contraction that historically leads broader economic downturns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; HOUST declines during low-rate periods do not carry the same signal because they are not policy-induced.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST falling to 1425k units alongside FEDFUNDS at 3.78% in late 2022
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails if forward 60-day SPX returns following triggered instances are not significantly more negative than returns following HOUST declines with FEDFUNDS <= 3% (difference-in-means p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals peaking industrial activity ahead of an economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets anticipate margin compression from falling capacity utilization.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5175 in Nov 2022 after a multi-year expansion phase
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM declines during late-cycle periods are followed by positive or flat 20-day SPX forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
